title: Profitability ratios
summary: 9609 A Level — gross profit margin, operating profit margin, ROCE, and trend analysis.
learningObjectives: Calculate gross profit margin and operating profit margin.
Link ratios to business performance in case studies.

simpleExplanation.title: How hard is money working?
simpleExplanation.summary: Profitability ratios show how much profit is earned relative to sales and capital invested — the core performance measures for directors and shareholders.

flashcards.back: Equity + Non-current liabilities (or Total assets − Current liabilities).
Rising COGS, price cuts, product mix shift, supplier cost increases.
Overheads rising — admin, marketing, wages not in COGS.
Operating profit (before finance costs) — measures return on all long-term capital.
Single-year ratio may be distorted; trends show direction of performance.

sections.content: **Profitability ratios** (10.2.2) use figures from **10.1.1**. Examiners often give **two years** — calculate both years and **comment on the trend**.

Gross profit margin = $\frac{\text{Gross profit}}{\text{Revenue}} \times 100\%$

Operating profit margin = $\frac{\text{Operating profit}}{\text{Revenue}} \times 100\%$

ROCE = $\frac{\text{Operating profit}}{\text{Capital employed}} \times 100\%$

sections.question:
Revenue $1 000 000; gross profit $350 000; operating profit $120 000; capital employed $600 000.

Calculate GPM, OPM, and ROCE.
GPM fell from 40% to 35% while OPM fell from 15% to 10%. Suggest two possible causes.
sections.solution:
**GPM fall:** higher cost of sales (material prices, inefficiency), **price reductions** to maintain volume, or shift to **lower-margin products**.

**OPM fell further than GPM:** **overheads increased** — e.g. higher admin wages, marketing spend, rent — eating into gross profit.

Use **case evidence** to pick the most likely cause.

sections.content: ROCE is often compared to **interest rate** or **cost of capital** — ROCE should exceed borrowing cost for gearing to be beneficial.
